channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: US seeks international coalition to secure shipping in Strait of Hormuz amid Middle East tensions
item.description: The US administration under President Donald Trump is reportedly planning to announce a coalition of countries to escort ships through the strategically important Strait of Hormuz. Trump emphasized th...

item.title: Two deaths and several cases reported in meningitis outbreak in Kent
item.description: Two individuals have died following an outbreak of meningitis in the Canterbury area of Kent, according to the UK Health Security Agency (UKHSA). Eleven more people have shown symptoms of meningitis a...

item.title: Practical Strategies for Enjoyable Family Mealtimes
item.description: Sharing meals as a family, even just a few nights a week, helps establish healthy eating habits, encourages positive social interaction, and supports emotional development in children. Preschool-aged ...

item.title: Exploring Neo Deco: Modern Takes on Classic Art Deco Design
item.description: The Neo Deco trend is emerging as one of the key interior design styles for 2026, evolving from the 1920s and 1930s art deco revival. Celebrities such as Zoe Saldaña have showcased this style, highlig...

item.title: How Agroforestry Can Create Jobs and Income for Tanzanian Youth and Women
item.description: In Tanzania and across Africa, job creation for the growing youth population is a big challenge. Millions of young people join the labour market every year, but formal jobs are few and very competitiv...

item.title: Pakistani representatives highlight challenges and progress for women’s rights at UN
item.description: At the 70th session of the Commission on the Status of Women (CSW70) at the United Nations, Pakistan’s delegation made a strong impression by putting women’s access to justice at the centre of the dis...

item.title: 'Sinners' Cast Dazzles at the 2026 Oscars as Record-Breaking Film Awaits Historic Wins
item.description: The cast of Sinners, the critically acclaimed and record-breaking film directed by Ryan Coogler, turned heads at the 98th Academy Awards with their elegant red carpet appearances. The film, set in the...

item.title: Udinese Coach Praises Maduka Okoye's Performance Despite 1-0 Defeat to Juventus
item.description: Nigerian international goalkeeper Maduka Okoye receive plenty praise from im head coach Kosta Runjaic even after Udinese suffer 1-0 defeat to Juventus for Bluenergy Stadium for Serie A match wey happe...

item.title: Chiefs edge Durban City 1-0 to halt losing streak
item.description: Kaizer Chiefs managed to halt a potential four-match losing streak by narrowly beating Durban City 1-0 on a chilly Sunday afternoon. The lone goal came from Glody Lilepo, who struck brilliantly after ...

item.title: ABS-CBN’s ‘Roja’ Finale Sets New Online Viewership Record
item.description: ABS-CBN’s hit TV series “Roja” has achieved a major milestone with its finale, breaking previous online viewership records in the Philippines. The show, which has captured the attention of audiences n...

item.title: US Defence Secretary’s Aggressive Rhetoric During Iran Conflict
item.description: The article analyses the rhetoric of US Secretary of Defence Pete Hegseth during the early stages of the US-Israeli operations in Iran in March 2026. Unlike previous officials such as James Mattis, wh...

item.title: Amy Madigan Gives Humorous and Down-to-Earth Response After Winning Best Supporting Actress Oscar
item.description: Amy Madigan, star of 'Weapons', responded with humour and charm following her win for Best Supporting Actress at the Academy Awards. When asked if she had prepared a speech in advance, she jokingly re...

item.title: Bangladesh Secure ODI Series Victory Against Pakistan
item.description: Well, phir kya baat hai! Bangladesh has snatched the three-match ODI series from Pakistan with a narrow 11-run victory at Mirpur’s Sher-e-Bangla National Cricket Stadium. The Green Shirts, who were ho...

item.title: FG Accuses Mining Firm Jupiter Ltd of Planning Smear Campaign Over Licence Revocation Ahead of Tinubu's UK Visit
item.description: The Federal Government, via the Ministry of Solid Minerals Development, don accuse one mining company wey dem call Jupiter Ltd say dem dey plan big campaign of calumny to spoil Nigeria name during Pre...

item.title: UEFA Cancels Argentina vs Spain Finalissima Amid Middle East Tensions
item.description: UEFA has officially cancelled the highly anticipated Finalissima match between Argentina and Spain, which was scheduled for March 27 at Doha’s Lusail Stadium in Qatar. The match, featuring South Ameri...

item.title: Ongoing Tensions and Escalation in the Middle East Amid US-Israel-Iran Conflict
item.description: As of March 16, 2026, tensions in the Middle East continue to escalate, particularly in Lebanon, due to recent clashes between Hezbollah and Israel. The conflict is occurring within the broader contex...

item.title: Database Manager Dismissed for Falsifying Work-from-Home Timesheets
item.description: An IT professional at Hansen Corporation was dismissed after it was discovered he had been falsifying his work-from-home timesheets. Despite claiming to work 7.5 hours per day, records showed he logge...

item.title: Ensuring Strong Governance to Protect Kenya’s Health System
item.description: The health sector in Kenya is at a critical point, and the Constitution of Kenya guarantees every citizen the right to the highest attainable standard of health, including access to reproductive and e...

item.title: CPM announces 86 candidates for Kerala assembly elections, LDF optimistic of victory
item.description: On 16th March 2026, the Communist Party of India (Marxist) announced that it will contest 86 assembly constituencies in Kerala for the upcoming elections. The party named 81 candidates, which include ...

item.title: SANDF Chief Declares Tough Stance Against Criminals During Deployment to Crime Hotspots
item.description: The Chief of the South African National Defence Force (SANDF), Gen Rudzani Maphwanya, has stated that soldiers deployed to assist in fighting crime will take decisive action against criminals and will...
